directives.note: Section 2 items[0] invents check-in workflow: 'Check-in is inside the lobby, and a teacher will walk you and your kid to the room.' No atom or fact carries this check-in procedure or teacher-walks-to-room handoff. Either route to content collection for Paradox's actual check-in process or drop the invented logistics and let the When/Where item carry just the time + address.

Section 2 repeats Saturday 9:15 AM + 51 W Olive + ages 0 to 4 across tagline + primary_heading + body + items[0].item_heading + items[0].item_body + items[1].item_heading. Within ONE section, the time appears 4x and the address 2x. Pick the slot each fact lands best (tagline for ages+time; items[0] for time+address) and drop the duplicates from body and primary_heading. The 'whole gathering follows at 10:00' belongs in section 3, not here.

Body claim 'The whole gathering follows at 10:00, so drop-off and pick-up stay simple' invents a drop-off/pick-up framing not present in any atom or fact. The 10:00 service time may be supported by fact 631e87bd, but the drop-off/pick-up workflow phrasing is unsourced. Trim to just the time fact or drop.

Validation flag is correct to surface: the hero body 'BEGINNING WITH A STRONG SENSE OF BELONGING' is verbatim atom 2528ca66 in all-caps, which renders as shouting in a hero body slot. Verbatim casing is preserved (correct), but strategist should confirm designer treats this as a styled kicker label, not body prose. Consider whether the atom is better bound to a 'tagline' or 'kicker' slot than 'body'.

problem_lines: ParaTots meets Saturdays at 9:15 AM at 51 W Olive in Redlands, for kids ages 0 to 4. Teacher Donna coordinates the room each week. The whole gathering follows at 10:00, so drop-off and pick-up stay simple.
Saturdays at 9:15 AM at 51 W Olive, Redlands. Check-in is inside the lobby, and a teacher will walk you and your kid to the room.
BEGINNING WITH A STRONG SENSE OF BELONGING
standout_lines: before you were even born, god loved you first
A quiet room off the gathering space with somewhere to sit, feed a baby, or settle a fussy one. If you need a minute to breathe, take it. Nobody is counting.
Raising kids under five is a mystery, and nobody here pretends otherwise. Kids are loud in the lobby and welcome to be. If you have questions about any of it, Teacher Donna reads every email.
Blocks, crayons, and floor time with teachers who know your kid by name. Belonging starts here, before any of it has words.
critique_for_inspection.summary: Voice is largely on — the hero, the Parents Room item, and the closing CTA echo the stage_1 exemplars cleanly (the 'Kids are loud in the lobby' line lands verbatim; 'Nobody is counting' is the right rhythm for this church). Verbatim atoms preserved exactly, including the all-caps belonging atom (validation flag is appropriate). Dignity floor is strong: no performed warmth, no village cliches, quiet solidarity in the close. Two real defects: (1) Section 2 items[0] invents a check-in workflow ('Check-in is inside the lobby, and a teacher will walk you and your kid to the room') with no source in atoms or facts — this is a logistic invention and drops claim_plausibility per the source-grounding procedure. (2) Section 2 repeats Saturday 9:15 + 51 W Olive across tagline + heading + body + two items — intra-section redundancy per the voice_character procedure; parents read the same fact four times in 200 words. Recommend kickback to drafter for surgical fixes on section 2. Personas (Lena/Marcus/Jordan) addressed indirectly through the 'generous, inclusive, affirming' triad and the no-performance posture — fits.
